title: Atelophragma shearii
taxonomy: Atelophragma shearii is a species of frog, and as such, it belongs to the class Amphibia. Specifically, it is a member of the family Bufonidae, which includes various species of true toads. Atelophragma shearii is further classified as a genus within the family Bufonidae, being the sole species within the genus Atelophragma. This classification places it in the order Anura, which comprises all the frog species. Atelophragma shearii's classification is a part of the broader taxonomic system used to categorize living organisms.
properties: Atelophragma shearii is a species of frog native to the tropical forests of Central America. However, information about the therapeutic properties of Atelophragma shearii is limited. Research has shown that some species of frogs, including Atelophragma shearii, have bioactive compounds that have potential therapeutic applications. These compounds may have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, similar to those found in other frog species. Further studies are needed to fully understand the therapeutic properties of Atelophragma shearii and its potential uses in medicine.
